title: Fine-tuning: full vs LoRA vs QLoRA + DSA review
focus: When FT beats RAG; LoRA decomposition; data curation for FT.

tracks.label: GenAI · Fine-tuning (full vs LoRA vs QLoRA)

items.label: When to fine-tune vs prompt vs RAG
interviewQuestions: When does fine-tuning beat RAG, and vice versa?
How would you decide between SFT, LoRA, preference tuning, prompt engineering, and RAG for a domain assistant?

items.label: LoRA, QLoRA: math and trade-offs
interviewQuestions: Walk through how LoRA decomposes weight updates into low-rank matrices.
What does QLoRA add on top of LoRA, and what does it cost?
How do you pick rank r and alpha for LoRA?

items.label: Fine-tuning data: curation, dedup, contamination
interviewQuestions: How would you curate 10k high-quality examples for fine-tuning a domain LLM?
What's your dedup + contamination check between training and eval sets — and why does it matter?
What data filters would you apply for unclear prompts, low-quality responses, semantic duplicates, and weak rater agreement?
How would you build capability-specific SFT data for code, safety, factuality, multilinguality, long-context, and tool use?
